# runtask-ui.yaml LLM Guide

This guide is only for `runtask-ui.yaml`, the configuration file for `runtask ui`.
It does not cover `.vscode/tasks.json`; that remains standard VS Code task configuration.

Important: `--runtime-mode=always-on|serverless` is a `runtask ui` startup flag, not a YAML field in `runtask-ui.yaml`.

## UI command limits

`runtask ui init`, `runtask ui edit task`, and `runtask ui edit branch` intentionally cover only basic setup:

- repository source
- allowed branches
- a simple task allowlist
- one artifact path per task
- basic auth mode
- basic storage mode

If the user needs advanced auth rules, token storage, object storage tuning, logging redaction, pre-run commands, schedules, heartbeat-driven serverless execution, or sparse/full worktree control, edit `runtask-ui.yaml` directly instead of trying to force those settings through the interactive UI commands.

If the user asks about shared-history live updates or serverless vs always-on browser behavior, also tell them to adjust the `runtask ui` startup flag:

- `runtask ui --runtime-mode=always-on`
- `runtask ui --runtime-mode=serverless`

## Required schema comment

When generating or rewriting `runtask-ui.yaml`, always put this exact comment on the first line:

```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shibukawa/vsc-taskrunner/main/config-schema.json
```

Use the comment form. Do not add a top-level `$schema` key.

## Top-level keys

- `server`
- `repository`
- `auth`
- `branches`
- `tasks`
- `execution`
- `metrics`
- `logging`
- `storage`

Only `repository.source` is always required. Most other settings have defaults in Go validation.

## Field reference

### `server`

- `host`: bind host, default `localhost`
- `port`: bind port, default `8080`, must be `1..65535`
- `publicURL`: absolute `http://` or `https://` URL, used for external links and OIDC callback generation

### `repository`

- `source`: local git path or remote repository URL, required
- `localPath`: optional local clone path for remote repositories
- `cachePath`: required by Go validation when `source` is remote
- `auth`: optional token validation settings for remote repositories

### `repository.auth`

- `type`: currently only `envToken`
- `provider`: `github`, `gitlab`, or `bitbucket`
- `tokenEnv`: environment variable containing the token
- `baseURL`: optional provider API base URL, mainly for self-managed GitLab
- `repo`: allowed repository identifier such as `owner/repo` or `group/project`
- `allowedHosts`: allowlist of remote/API hosts
- `rejectBroadScope`: default `true`
- `requireReadOnly`: default `true`

### `auth`

- `noAuth`: disable authentication entirely
- `oidcIssuer`, `oidcClientID`, `oidcClientSecret`: OIDC settings
- `sessionSecret`: session signing secret
- `allowUsers`: mapping from claim to glob or list of globs
- `adminUsers`: same shape as `allowUsers`
- `apiTokens`: admin-issued API token settings

### `auth.apiTokens`

- `enabled`: requires `auth.noAuth=false`
- `defaultTTLHours`: default `720`
- `maxPerUser`: default `10`
- `store.backend`: `local` or `object`
- `store.localPath`: default `.runtask/api-tokens.json`
- `store.object.*`: separate object storage settings for token persistence

### `branches`

- List of allowed branch patterns
- If omitted or empty, only top-level local branches are allowed

### `tasks`

`tasks` is a mapping keyed by task-label glob pattern. The first matching pattern wins, so order matters.

Each task config may contain:

- `artifacts`
- `preRunTask`
- `schedules`
- `worktree.disabled`
- `worktree.keepOnSuccess`
- `worktree.keepOnFailure`

### `tasks.<pattern>.artifacts[]`

- `path`: required path or glob to collect
- `format`: `zip` or `file`, default `zip`
- `nameTemplate`: archive filename template for `format: zip`; default `artifacts.zip`

Notes:

- `format: zip` creates one archive per rule
- `format: file` keeps matching files individually using each matched relative path as the output name
- duplicate matches across rules are allowed
- `nameTemplate` also supports `{input:NAME}` placeholders using sanitized task input values

### `tasks.<pattern>.preRunTask[]`

- `command`: required
- `args`: optional string list
- `cwd`: optional working directory
- `env`: optional string map
- `shell.executable`: required only when `shell` is set
- `shell.args`: optional shell args

Behavior:

- pre-run commands execute once before the selected parent task
- they are written into the combined log
- they are not stored as child tasks
- if a pre-run command fails, the parent task does not start

### `tasks.<pattern>.schedules[]`

- `cron`: required standard 5-field cron expression
- `branch`: required branch name for the scheduled run
- `inputValues`: optional fixed string map for task inputs

Behavior:

- schedule branches must also pass the top-level `branches` allowlist
- cron is evaluated in the server's local time zone
- in always-on mode, runtask evaluates schedules automatically in the background
- in serverless mode, schedules advance only when `/api/heartbeat` is called
- late heartbeat calls collapse multiple missed slots into one run for the latest due slot

Runtime-mode notes outside YAML:

- `runtask ui --runtime-mode=always-on` enables the global `/api/runs/stream` SSE endpoint for immediate shared-history updates
- `runtask ui --runtime-mode=serverless` disables the global run stream; browsers rely on conditional polling of `GET /api/runs`
- default runtime mode is `always-on`

### `tasks.<pattern>.worktree.disabled`

- default `false`
- when `true`, the task runs in the sparse tasks-only workspace instead of the full worktree

### `execution`

- `maxParallelRuns`: default `4`
- `0` means unlimited

### `metrics`

- `enabled`: default `true`
- `cpuInterval`: default `5`
- `memoryInterval`: default `15`
- `storageInterval`: default `60`
- `memoryHistoryWindow`: default `300`

All metric intervals must be positive.

### `logging`

- `redaction.names`: extra secret-like names to redact
- `redaction.tokens`: extra literal tokens to redact

### `storage`

- `backend`: `local` or `object`, default `local`
- `historyDir`: default `.runtask/history`
- `historyKeepCount`: default `100`
- `object.*`: object storage config used when `backend: object`
- `worktree.keepOnSuccess`: default `0`
- `worktree.keepOnFailure`: default `3`

Object storage requires at least:

- `endpoint`
- `bucket`

`region` defaults to `us-east-1`.

## Generation rules

When generating YAML:

- Put the schema comment on line 1.
- Prefer omitting fields that are equal to defaults unless the user explicitly wants them shown.
- Use placeholder secrets like `${OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET}` instead of real secret values.
- Keep `tasks` entries in priority order because first match wins.
- Do not assume `preRunTask` becomes a visible child task.
- Do not assume `worktree.disabled: true` means “disabled task”; it means sparse workspace execution.
- Do not treat `/api/heartbeat` as a generic remote-run API; it only evaluates schedules already defined in YAML.
- For remote repositories, remember that Go validation may still require `cachePath` even if the schema cannot fully express that cross-field rule.
- For `auth.apiTokens.enabled: true`, remember Go validation also requires `auth.noAuth=false`.

## Common patterns

### Minimal local repo

```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shibukawa/vsc-taskrunner/main/config-schema.json
repository:
  source: /srv/repos/demo
auth:
  noAuth: true
tasks:
  build: {}
```

### OIDC with admin token issuance

```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shibukawa/vsc-taskrunner/main/config-schema.json
server:
  publicURL: https://runtask.example.com
repository:
  source: https://github.com/acme/demo.git
  cachePath: /var/lib/runtask/demo-cache.git
  auth:
    type: envToken
    provider: github
    tokenEnv: GITHUB_FINE_GRAINED_TOKEN
    repo: acme/demo
    allowedHosts:
      - github.com
      - api.github.com
auth:
  oidcIssuer: https://issuer.example.com
  oidcClientID: runtask
  oidcClientSecret: ${OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET}
  sessionSecret: ${SESSION_SECRET}
  adminUsers:
    role:
      - administrator
  apiTokens:
    enabled: true
tasks:
  deploy: {}
```

### Artifact collection with pre-run install

```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shibukawa/vsc-taskrunner/main/config-schema.json
repository:
  source: /srv/repos/frontend
auth:
  noAuth: true
tasks:
  build:
    artifacts:
      - path: dist
        format: zip
        nameTemplate: frontend-{branch}-{yyyymmdd}-{hhmmss}-{hash}.zip
    preRunTask:
      - command: npm
        args:
          - ci
        cwd: ${workspaceFolder}
```

### Scheduled build in serverless mode

```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shibukawa/vsc-taskrunner/main/config-schema.json
repository:
  source: https://github.com/acme/demo.git
  cachePath: /var/lib/runtask/demo-cache.git
auth:
  noAuth: true
branches:
  - main
tasks:
  build:
    schedules:
      - cron: "0 6 * * 1-5"
        branch: main
        inputValues:
          env: staging
storage:
  backend: object
  object:
    endpoint: https://s3.example.com
    bucket: runtask
```

Trigger from an external scheduler with:

```sh
curl -fsS https://runtask.example.com/api/heartbeat
```

## Related files

- Schema: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shibukawa/vsc-taskrunner/main/config-schema.json`
- Repository documentation: consult repository-local README or Web UI documentation when available
- Repository customization: consult any repository-local skill or prompt for direct `runtask-ui.yaml` editing when available

Go validation in the runtask codebase remains the final source of truth when schema and prose are not enough to express a rule.
